questions.question: What is the primary way a population becomes 'Human Capital'?

options.B: Through investment in education, training, and medical care

questions.explanation: Human capital is the stock of skill and productive knowledge embodied in a population through education and health.

questions.question: Human resource is considered superior to land and physical capital because:

options.B: It can make use of land and capital

questions.explanation: Land and capital cannot become useful on their own; they require human effort and knowledge to produce value.

questions.question: In the story of Sakal and Vilas, why did Sakal earn more in the long run?

options.B: He received education and vocational training
options.C: He inherited a lot of land
options.D: He worked in his father's field

questions.explanation: Sakal's investment in education and a course in computers led to a high-paying job in a private firm.

questions.question: Activities that result in pay or profit are called:
questions.explanation: Market activities involve remuneration to anyone who performs them for pay or profit.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a 'Primary Sector' activity?
questions.explanation: The primary sector includes activities related to natural resources like agriculture, forestry, and fishing.

questions.question: Manufacturing and construction fall under which sector?
questions.explanation: The secondary sector involves the processing of raw materials into finished goods.

questions.question: Trade, transport, banking, and health are parts of the:
questions.explanation: The tertiary sector provides services that support the production of goods in other sectors.

questions.question: Economic activities performed for self-consumption are called:
questions.explanation: Non-market activities are the production for self-consumption, such as a farmer growing food for his own family.

questions.question: What is the major determinant of an individual's earnings in the market?
questions.explanation: Education and skill are the primary factors that determine the quality of labor and its price in the market.

questions.question: The quality of the population depends on which factor?
questions.explanation: A healthy, educated, and skilled population determines the growth rate of a country.

questions.question: What is the aim of 'Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan'?

options.B: Providing elementary education to all children aged 6-14

questions.explanation: It is a time-bound initiative of the central government to achieve universal elementary education.

questions.question: The Mid-day Meal scheme was implemented in schools to:

options.C: Improve the nutritional status of children and encourage attendance

questions.explanation: Cooked meals in schools help improve health and retention of students, especially from poor backgrounds.

questions.question: Which indicator shows an improvement in the health status of the population?
questions.explanation: Longer life expectancy indicates better nutrition, medical facilities, and overall standard of living.

questions.question: Unemployment is said to exist when:

options.A: Children are not going to school
options.B: People are willing to work at prevailing wages but cannot find jobs
options.C: People choose to stay at home for leisure

questions.explanation: The textbook defines unemployment based on the willingness to work and the inability to find employment in the workforce age group.

questions.question: The workforce population includes people in the age group of:
questions.explanation: The productive age group considered in economic statistics for India is 15-59 years.

questions.question: What is 'Seasonal Unemployment'?

options.A: When people are jobless during certain months of the year
options.B: When people are employed only during winter
options.C: When people are replaced by machines
options.D: When educated people don't find jobs

questions.explanation: This is common in agriculture where work is available during sowing or harvesting but not throughout the year.

questions.question: Disguised unemployment is most common in which sector?
questions.explanation: It occurs when more people are working on a farm than are actually required.

questions.question: Educated unemployment is a common phenomenon in:
questions.explanation: Urban areas often have matriculates and graduates who cannot find suitable employment.

questions.question: Unemployment leads to 'Economic Overload' because:

options.A: The unemployed become a burden on the working population
options.B: It increases the number of bank accounts

questions.explanation: The dependency of the unemployed on the working members of society increases.

questions.question: Health helps a person to realize his/her potential and the ability to:
questions.explanation: A healthy person is an asset to an organization, while an unhealthy one becomes a liability.

questions.question: A country's working people are referred to as 'People as Resource' because of their:
questions.explanation: The term emphasizes the productive potential of the population.

questions.question: Which of these countries has invested heavily in human resources despite lacking natural resources?
questions.explanation: Japan became a rich, developed country by investing in education and health for its people.

questions.question: In the case of Vilas, his inability to go to school led to:

options.B: Continuing his mother's work of selling fish

questions.explanation: Lack of education and health care trapped Vilas in the same low-income cycle as his parents.

questions.question: Which sector has the highest absorption of labour in India?
questions.explanation: Historically and currently, agriculture remains the largest employer in India.

questions.question: The 'Virtuous Cycle' of human development is created by:

options.B: Educated parents investing in their children's schooling and health

questions.explanation: Educated parents realize the value of education and ensure their children receive it, passing on the benefit.
